cases.prompt: I want to add rate limiting to our public REST API. Before you write any code, put together a plan for this work.
cases.expected_behavior: The assistant invokes the plan skill and produces a single markdown plan document containing all five sections: objective function with mechanically verifiable success criteria (concrete commands or checks, not subjective statements), feasible region with hard constraints, soft constraints, and anti-constraints, permission architecture with in/out of scope and autonomous vs confirm-first vs prohibited actions, feedback loop including verification commands and a stop-after-3-identical-failures rule, and context persistence naming what goes to CLAUDE.md or ADRs. No implementation code is written in the same response.
assertions: The response contains a markdown document with headings for all five sections: objective function, feasible region, permission architecture, feedback loop, and context persistence.

Every success criterion in the objective function section is stated as a runnable command or mechanically checkable condition (e.g., a test command, grep, or HTTP status check), with no criterion phrased only as a subjective judgment.
The feedback loop section includes an explicit rule to stop and escalate after 3 identical failures.
No source code files are created or edited in the transcript before the plan is presented.

cases.prompt: Plan a migration of our user service from MySQL to PostgreSQL.
cases.expected_behavior: The assistant invokes the plan skill and, because key facts (downtime tolerance, data volume, cutover strategy, compatibility requirements) are unknowable from the prompt alone, either asks targeted clarifying questions before finalizing constraints or explicitly marks unknown constraints as open questions rather than inventing specific values. The plan still follows the five-section structure, and the feasible region distinguishes hard constraints, soft constraints, and anti-constraints (explicit freedoms).
assertions: The transcript contains either clarifying questions to the user or an explicit list of open questions/assumptions, rather than fabricated specific constraints (e.g., invented downtime windows or row counts stated as fact).
The feasible region section labels three distinct constraint classes: hard constraints, soft constraints, and anti-constraints.
The permission architecture section lists at least one prohibited action and at least one confirm-first action.
The plan is delivered as one markdown document, not split across multiple separate plan files or formats.

cases.prompt: We keep re-explaining our build quirks to every new Claude session. Plan the work to add a caching layer to the asset pipeline, and make sure the plan covers what we should write down for future sessions.
cases.expected_behavior: The assistant invokes the plan skill and produces the five-section plan with particular attention to the context persistence section: it names concrete facts or conventions destined for CLAUDE.md, identifies decisions that warrant an ADR (with title/status/decision/consequences structure), and notes anything intentionally not persisted. Verification commands in the feedback loop are concrete and ordered.
assertions: The context persistence section explicitly mentions both CLAUDE.md and ADRs as persistence targets with at least one concrete item assigned to each.
The feedback loop section lists at least two concrete verification commands to run after changes.
The plan contains all five section headings in a single markdown document.
The objective function section contains at least two success criteria expressed as commands or mechanically checkable conditions.

cases.prompt: There's a typo in the README: 'recieve' should be 'receive'. Fix it.
cases.expected_behavior: The assistant does not invoke the plan skill. It simply fixes the typo directly (edits the README) without producing a five-section constraint-programming plan, because the task is a trivial one-line fix outside the skill's stated scope.
assertions: The plan skill is not invoked in the transcript.
No five-section plan document (objective function / feasible region / permission architecture / feedback loop / context persistence) appears in the response.
The README typo is corrected via a direct file edit.
